mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding aggregate income and consumption demand, which drives corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year window or positive return rate below 55% during PAYEMS expansion months.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, restrictive monetary policy elevates corporate borrowing costs and compresses valuation multiples, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In triggered periods (FEDFUNDS > 4.5%), the negative return hit rate is below 50% or mean 20d return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month contraction in industrial production alongside a rising unemployment rate signals a synchronous supply-demand downturn that amplifies earnings risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint signal Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window or the interaction term does not outperform individual series in linear projection.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts during a late-cycle expansion, characterized by high payroll employment, forecast a contraction in construction-related demand and durable goods spending, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: During late-cycle expansions (PAYEMS > 130000), HOUST declines fail to produce negative 60d returns in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating retail sales signal robust consumer expenditure, which translates into higher corporate top-line growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window or positive return rate below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid 0.3 percentage point rise in the unemployment rate over a three-month window signals labor market deterioration that reduces household income growth and triggers risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When triggered, negative return hit rate is below 50% or mean 20d return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: An elevated federal funds rate combined with accelerating consumer price inflation signals aggressive monetary tightening to contain price pressures, which jointly compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint signal Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power over the main effects.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth below 1.5% annualized signals stagnating aggregate demand that undermines corporate profit forecasts, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In triggered quarters, negative 60d return hit rate is below 50% or mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).
